hook: You already know how to work with lines and parabolas — but most real-world relationships are far more complex than those simple curves. Polynomial, power, and rational functions give you a much richer toolkit for modeling everything from roller coaster designs to population growth and drug concentrations in the bloodstream. What does a function's equation secretly tell you about its graph — before you ever plot a single point?
concept: Every topic in this unit is about deeply understanding the behavior of functions built from powers and ratios — not just evaluating them, but predicting, factoring, graphing, and solving them with confidence. The unifying idea is the structure of polynomials and rational expressions as algebraic objects whose properties (zeros, end behavior, asymptotes) are directly encoded in their equations.

topic_roadmap.description: You will learn to read a polynomial's degree and leading coefficient to predict how its graph rises and falls at the far left and far right.

topic_roadmap.topic_name: Dividing Polynomials and the Remainder Theorem
topic_roadmap.description: Master polynomial long division and synthetic division, and use the Remainder Theorem to quickly evaluate polynomials and test potential zeros.
Apply the Rational Zero Theorem and factoring strategies to locate all real zeros of a polynomial and connect those zeros to x-intercepts on the graph.

topic_roadmap.topic_name: Complex Zeros and the Fundamental Theorem of Algebra
topic_roadmap.description: You will learn why every polynomial of degree $n$ has exactly $n$ zeros (counting multiplicity) in the complex numbers, and how complex zeros always appear in conjugate pairs.
Identify vertical and horizontal asymptotes, holes, and intercepts of rational functions and use them to sketch accurate, complete graphs.
Apply sign-chart analysis to solve polynomial and rational inequalities and express solution sets accurately using interval notation.
You will learn to recognize power function models of the form $y = ax^b$, fit them to real data, and interpret regression results in context.
